new_files.note: Shared M6 core: intrusive free-frame stack + reservation algebra + guarded kernel-image linker-symbol read + identity-mapped frame-link read/write + safe crate-internal facade + pmm_selftest. FIXES vs draft: (1) MAJOR — frame_in_usable now uses checked_add so a 4 KiB-aligned PA near u64::MAX is rejected fail-closed instead of overflowing (debug panic / release wrap) on the trusted frame_free boundary; is_reserved_frame uses saturating_add. (2) RegionSink reservation-table overflow now fails CLOSED (init refuses to seed, total_frames==0) instead of silently dropping a reservation. (3) Module doc documents the O(1) intrusive-stack double-free limitation (mid-stack double-free undetectable, by design) — kept O(1) per the milestone requirement.

x86_64 boot memory-map reader (PVH hvm_start_info.memmap + tb-boot TbBootInfo). FIXES vs draft: (1) PVH memmap walk now gated on hvm_start_info version>=1 (memmap_paddr@40/memmap_entries@48 are version>=1-only; v0 fail-closes). (2) Boot-module PAYLOADS are now reserved (walk hvm_modlist_entry paddr@0/size@8, 32B stride) so initrd-class type==1 RAM is never handed out. (3) Data-derived pointers (regions_ptr/memmap/modlist) use saturating_add so a foreign block carrying the PVH magic + garbage pointers fail-closes instead of debug-panicking — making the 'a stray pointer can never fault the boot path' guarantee actually hold. (4) Softened the cross-path-equality wording (converge, not bit-identical) and documented the <1 GiB start_info/memmap reachability assumption.

aarch64 boot memory-map source (QEMU virt) — UNCHANGED from the reviewed draft (it was correct). ROADMAP-blessed hard-coded RAM map [0x4000_0000,+128 MiB) clamped to the M3 identity RAM gigabyte [0x4000_0000,0x8000_0000); device MMIO (PL011/GIC/virtio-mmio, all <0x4000_0000) excluded naturally by the RAM-range restriction; DTB reserved precisely via the big-endian FDT header (magic 0xD00DFEED, totalsize@4) read from the x0 pointer, plus the low-RAM staging window below the image. read_be32 already uses checked_add, so no overflow hardening needed here.

edits.new_string:
[dependencies]
# M6: the shared boot-handoff ABI (no_std, forbid(unsafe_code)). tb-hal's
# x86_64 frame-allocator boot-map reader parses a tb-vmm `TbBootInfo` using the
# crate's typed `#[repr(C)]` readers + constants instead of hand-coded offsets.
# No cycle: tb-boot depends on nothing; the kernel already depends on both.
tb-boot = { path = "../tb-boot" }
edits.note: Add the tb-boot path dependency so the x86_64 reader uses TbBootInfo/TbMemRegion typed parsers + constants. tb-boot is no_std/forbid(unsafe); no cycle; unused on aarch64 (allow-by-default, no [lints]/-Dwarnings).

edits.new_string:
mod arch;
mod heap; // M5: free-list global allocator over a fixed .bss arena.
mod mmu; // M3: shared typed page-table layer (PageTable512/Frame4K + entry math).
mod pmm; // M6: intrusive free-frame physical allocator over the boot memory map.
edits.note: Declare the new private shared M6 module.
Append the safe M6 public facade (every pub item documented; tb-hal denies missing_docs). Distinct region from the `mod pmm;` edit so both anchors survive a pristine apply.
Re-export the uniform arch contract fn pmm_collect_regions(boot_info, &mut crate::pmm::RegionSink) from both arches so crate::pmm::init can call crate::arch::pmm_collect_regions.

edits.old_string:
pub mod user;

pub use serial::{serial_init, serial_write_byte};
edits.note: Declare the x86_64 pmm submodule (same pub mod style as its siblings).

edits.old_string:
pub use user::user_demo;

use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};
edits.note: Re-export pmm_collect_regions at the x86_64 module level for arch/mod.rs to forward. Different region from the `pub mod pmm;` edit; both anchors survive a pristine apply.
Declare the aarch64 pmm submodule (private, matching sibling style) and re-export pmm_collect_regions.

edits.old_string:
* Symbols exported for crates/tb-hal/src/arch/aarch64/boot.rs (`_start`):
 *   __bss_start, __bss_end        -- .bss bounds, zeroed at boot
 *   __boot_stack_top              -- initial SP (16 KiB stack, grows down)
 *   __exception_vectors           -- 2 KiB-aligned VBAR_EL1 table (in .text)
 */
edits.new_string:
* Symbols exported for crates/tb-hal/src/arch/aarch64/boot.rs (`_start`):
 *   __bss_start, __bss_end        -- .bss bounds, zeroed at boot
 *   __boot_stack_top              -- initial SP (16 KiB stack, grows down)
 *   __exception_vectors           -- 2 KiB-aligned VBAR_EL1 table (in .text)
 * Symbols exported for crates/tb-hal/src/pmm.rs (M6 frame allocator):
 *   __image_start, __image_end    -- whole-image bounds; the frame allocator
 *                                    reserves [__image_start, __image_end) so it
 *                                    never hands out the image, its .bss (which
 *                                    holds M5's 2 MiB heap arena), or stacks.
 */
edits.note: Document the two new image-bound symbols (x86_64.ld already exports them; the shared pmm.rs reads __image_start/__image_end uniformly on both arches). Distinct region from the two symbol edits below.
Export __image_start at the image load base (0x4008_0000), matching x86_64.ld's __image_start at 1 MiB.
Export __image_end after .bss (which holds the 2 MiB heap arena + boot stack), so [__image_start,__image_end) covers the whole image. Mirrors x86_64.ld.

Insert the M6 self-test after the M5 marker, before the final halt. Zero unsafe: the kernel only calls the safe facade and compares u64 PAs. Covers alloc K + align/usable/reserved/disjoint, LIFO reuse, double-free/misaligned/null rejection, full reclaim to the seed-count invariant, pmm_selftest (through-frame magic), and drive-to-exhaustion -> None. Only heap use is one K=64 Vec, dropped before exhaustion.
DoD wiring: the PVH/QEMU runner now asserts the M6 marker (cumulative boot still prints M0-M5 earlier).
DoD wiring: the tb-vmm (tb-boot v0) runner asserts the M6 marker, proving the SAME usable set comes out of the TbBootInfo path.

NOTE: pristine-tree old_string is MARKER="M5: alloc OK" -> MARKER="M6: frame alloc OK". Shown here is the FINAL value; the orchestrator applies M5->M6 on the pristine tree. (Double-quoted in this script, unlike the single-quoted x86_64 runners.) DoD wiring: the QEMU virt aarch64 runner asserts the M6 marker.

Sound minor (review-flagged drift): advance the boot+self-test benchmark's terminal marker to M6 so it times the full M6 run, not just through M5 (the M5 marker still prints earlier in the cumulative boot, so it was non-gating but short).

config_changes: One Cargo.toml change; no .cargo/config.toml (kbuild alias), rust-toolchain, or feature-attr change. crates/tb-hal/Cargo.toml gains `tb-boot = { path = \"../tb-boot\" }` (the `[dependencies]` table was previously `# Intentionally empty at M0.`). Justification against the pinned nightly: the x86_64 boot-map reader parses a tb-vmm `TbBootInfo` using tb-boot's OWN typed `#[repr(C)]` readers + constants (TbBootInfo::SIZE/read_validated, TbMemRegion::SIZE/read_from_prefix/is_ram, TB_BOOT_MAGIC) rather than hand-coded offsets — verified against crates/tb-boot/src/lib.rs and its offset_of! tests. No dependency cycle: tb-boot depends on nothing (it is `#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)] #![forbid(unsafe_code)]`); the kernel already depends on both tb-hal and tb-boot (main.rs uses tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC). Under the pinned nightly + `cargo kbuild` (-Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc -Zbuild-std-features=compiler-builtins-mem -Zjson-target-spec) it builds no_std cleanly. tb_boot is referenced only from arch/x86_64/pmm.rs, so the aarch64 build leaves the crate unused; `unused_crate_dependencies` is allow-by-default and tb-hal does not deny it (and there is no `-D warnings` in .cargo/config.toml rustflags), so the aarch64 build is unaffected. NO tb-boot v0->v1 bump — TbBootInfo v0 already carries the RAM/Reserved region array M6 reads. The `alloc` build-std crate M5 added is reused unchanged (the M6 self-test uses only a tiny Vec<u64>). tb-hal keeps `#![deny(missing_docs)]`: every new crate-public pub item (the lib.rs facade) has a `///`; the new private/crate-internal modules (`mod pmm;` and the arch `*::pmm`) expose only pub(crate)/pub items reachable solely through the private `mod arch`, so they are not externally reachable and do not trip missing_docs. The `pub(crate) RegionSink` appears in `pub fn pmm_collect_regions` only inside the private `mod arch` subtree, so its effective visibility is capped at pub(crate) — no private_interfaces error, and at worst a non-fatal warn with no -Dwarnings.
arch_fallback:
aarch64 uses the HARD-CODED QEMU `virt` map (the ROADMAP-V2 M6 risk-note fallback), NOT a from-scratch FDT node walker. In crates/tb-hal/src/arch/aarch64/pmm.rs: usable RAM = [0x4000_0000, 0x4000_0000 + 128 MiB) (the runner's `-m 128M`), CLAMPED to the M3 identity-mapped RAM gigabyte [0x4000_0000, 0x8000_0000) — the only RAM the M3 L1[1] Normal-WB block identity-maps and the only window where the intrusive next-PA links can be written through identity addresses. Because usable RAM is RANGE-restricted to DRAM, every device MMIO window is excluded NATURALLY: PL011 @0x0900_0000, the GIC, and virtio-mmio all sit below 0x4000_0000 and can never be handed out. The DTB is still reserved PRECISELY (not hand-waved): the reader validates the big-endian FDT header magic@0 == 0xD00DFEED and reads totalsize@4 (guarded to the identity RAM gigabyte via checked_add) straight from the x0 FDT pointer, reserving exactly [fdt, fdt+totalsize), plus the conventional low-RAM staging window [0x4000_0000, 0x4008_0000) below the kernel image (KERNEL_LMA). A full /memory + /reserved-memory node parse is the post-M6 upgrade; the hard-coded map is the correct, bounded answer for the virt runner and equals what a correct /memory parse would yield. (Known coupling, documented: DRAM_SIZE=128 MiB tracks the runner's -m 128M; lowering -m without editing this would write links to mapped-but-unbacked frames — the post-M6 FDT parse removes it.) x86_64 is NOT a fallback: it does a real PVH hvm_start_info.memmap walk (type==1 = RAM, now GATED on hvm_start_info version>=1 since memmap_paddr@40/memmap_entries@48 are version>=1-only; module payloads reserved via hvm_modlist_entry) AND a real tb-boot TbBootInfo/TbMemRegion parse via the tb-boot crate's typed readers, both clamped to the [0,1 GiB) identity window, with all data-derived pointer arithmetic saturating so a foreign block can never fault.</arch_fallback>
<parameter name="rationale">DESIGN. M6 is an INTRUSIVE FREE-FRAME STACK (ROADMAP-V2): each free 4 KiB frame stores the next free PA in its own first 8 bytes, written through the frame's identity-mapped address (PA==VA in the M3 identity region). head is the stack top; alloc=O(1) pop, free=O(1) push, zero bitmap RAM. crate::pmm owns the stack + reservation algebra + the kernel-image linker-symbol read + the identity-mapped link read/write; per-arch crate::arch::*::pmm own only the boot-map READERS and push (usable RAM, reservations) into a RegionSink. State is guarded exactly like M5's heap (UnsafeCell<PmmState> + AtomicBool, single-core/non-preemptible; M8 must add interrupt-masking, same note as heap.rs). Framekernel rule honored absolutely: ALL raw reads/writes are in tb-hal; the kernel (kernel/src/main.rs) has zero `unsafe {}` and only compares returned u64 PAs.

FIXES APPLIED OVER THE DRAFT. (MAJOR) PmmState::frame_in_usable computed `pa + FRAME` unchecked; a 4 KiB-aligned PA near u64::MAX survived the align/null gate and overflowed — a debug-profile panic (the runners are debug) or a release wrap that could spuriously pass `pe <= r.end()` and write a link through an unmapped VA, the one stated 'frame_free rejects out-of-range' guarantee not met. Now uses checked_add -> reject fail-closed; is_reserved_frame uses saturating_add; and the readers' data-derived pointer arithmetic (regions_ptr/memmap/modlist walks) uses saturating_add so the 'a stray/foreign pointer can never fault the boot path' claim actually holds. (MINOR, hardening) RegionSink reservation-table overflow now fails CLOSED: push_reserved sets an overflow flag when MAX_RESV is exceeded and init refuses to seed (total_frames stays 0), instead of silently dropping a reservation that could then be handed out (usable-table overflow still silently drops, which is fail-safe). (MINOR) PVH memmap is now gated on hvm_start_info version>=1 (memmap_paddr@40/memmap_entries@48 don't exist in v0); and boot-module PAYLOADS are reserved by walking hvm_modlist_entry {paddr@0,size@8} (32B), since module RAM is reported type==1 and would otherwise be seeded. KEPT (review wrong / O(1) required): no full-scan mid-stack double-free guard — that would break the milestone's required O(1) free; the limitation is documented and unreachable in M6 (no kernel path frees a frame it does not hold).

MEMMAP SOURCES (verified against the repo). x86_64 distinguishes the two paths by the offset-0 magic, exactly like read_boot_magic: tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC -> parse TbBootInfo via the crate's typed readers (read_validated, TbMemRegion::read_from_prefix/is_ram; ptr/len at offsets 16/24, confirmed by tb-boot's offset_of! tests); else PVH hvm_start_info (version@4, nr_modules@12, modlist@16, cmdline@24, memmap_paddr@40, memmap_entries@48; each entry addr@0/size@8/type@16, 24 bytes; type==1=RAM — confirmed against the Xen start_info.h ABI and the in-repo PVH note emitter). All reads are byte-wise volatile, guarded to [0x1000,1 GiB), so a foreign pointer fail-closes (the docs now state the <1 GiB start_info/memmap reachability assumption explicitly). aarch64 uses the hard-coded virt map and reserves the DTB precisely from the big-endian FDT header.

SAME USABLE SET FROM BOTH x86_64 SOURCES (claim softened to 'converge', not 'guaranteed bit-identical'). Verified: tb-vmm emits ONE RAM region [0, mem_bytes) (memory.rs::GuestRam::new) and places GDT@0x500..PDE@0xb000, TbBootInfo@0x6000, regions@0x6040, cmdline@0x6800 ALL below 1 MiB (tb-vmm/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs); default --mem-mb 256, and the QEMU runner uses -m 256M, so both default to a 256 MiB guest. Both readers reserve, identically: sub-1 MiB (pushed unconditionally, subsuming every tb-vmm boot structure and its live CR3 tables at 0x9000-0xb000), the kernel image [__image_start,__image_end) (added once in the shared layer, the SAME ELF on both paths), and the parsed boot structures. For 256 MiB both reduce to [__image_end, 256 MiB) — PVH may additionally carve a few E820-reserved holes, so the sets COINCIDE rather than being bit-identical, and the marker test does not assert cross-path equality. The clamp to [0,1 GiB) confines usable RAM to the M3 identity map so a larger -m can never seed a frame whose link write would fault.

RESERVATION CORRECTNESS. The kernel-image span is the linchpin: in BOTH linker scripts the .bss output section (which contains M5's 2 MiB heap arena, the x86_64 boot page tables in .boot.pagetables, and the boot stack in .boot.stack) sits inside [__image_start,__image_end) (x86_64.ld already exported them; aarch64.ld now does too), so reserving that one span keeps the allocator off the live CR3 tables, the heap arena, the M3 test tables/frames, the M4 user pages, and tb-hal's own .bss statics. is_reserved_frame() excludes any frame intersecting ANY reservation at frame granularity (reservations need not be frame-aligned). aarch64 additionally reserves the low-RAM DTB staging window below the image and the exact [fdt,fdt+totalsize); device MMIO is excluded because usable RAM is RAM-range-restricted (all aarch64 MMIO is below DRAM base 0x4000_0000).

FRAMEKERNEL PLACEMENT. New unsafe is exactly: the three boot-map byte readers (arch::*::pmm), the identity-mapped frame link read/write and the kernel-image addr_of! (crate::pmm). The kernel's M6 block is pure safe Rust calling pmm_init/frame_alloc/frame_free/pmm_total_frames/pmm_free_frames/pmm_addr_in_usable_ram/pmm_addr_reserved/pmm_selftest. The forbidden write-through-the-frame check lives in pmm::selftest() (mirror of heap_selftest).

SELF-TEST -> EACH PROPERTY. (1) alloc 64: each PA asserted 4 KiB-aligned + pmm_addr_in_usable_ram + !pmm_addr_reserved + pairwise-distinct (distinct 4 KiB-aligned PAs are disjoint); free_count == total-64 proves the seed-count stat. (2) free the top then alloc -> same PA proves LIFO stack reuse. (3) double-free (pa==head) rejected, misaligned (pa+1) rejected, null (0) rejected, all fail-closed; reclaim of the remaining 63 returns free_count to total, proving the seed-count invariant round-trips. (4) pmm_selftest writes a magic through the frame's identity address (first + last word) and reads it back — the raw-memory proof the kernel cannot do. (5) drive-to-exhaustion drains exactly free_before frames and the next alloc returns None (fail-closed) with free_count==0; M6 is the last milestone before halt so the drain need not reclaim. The only heap use is one K=64 Vec<u64>, dropped before exhaustion. M0-M5 are untouched (the M5 marker still prints; the runners now grep the M6 marker; bench-boot.sh's terminal marker advanced to M6 too).
